title: Stand up an inference server
description: Wrap a model artifact in a serving runtime with batching, health/readiness probes, versioning, and metrics from the start.
body: Steps: 1. Load the model artifact into a serving runtime (Triton, vLLM, TorchServe, or a custom server). 2. Configure dynamic batching with a max size and queue timeout. 3. Add health and readiness probes gated on a warm-up inference. 4. Expose latency/throughput/utilization metrics and a model-version label. 5. Load-test before exposing traffic. Rollback point: keep the prior server/version image to revert. Validation: probes reflect real readiness, metrics emit, and a load test meets the SLO; a server with a liveness probe that passes before the model loads will route traffic to a cold instance.
